stem: A teacher is showing how merge sort orders the list [9, 5, 7, 1, 3, 8] into ascending order.
model_answer: Divide step: split the list in half, repeatedly, until each sub-list contains a single element: [9],[5],[7],[1],[3],[8]. Conquer step: merge pairs of sub-lists in order to produce sorted longer sub-lists: [9]+[5] → [5,9]; [7]+[1] → [1,7]; [3]+[8] → [3,8]. Merge again: [5,9]+[1,7] → [1,5,7,9] and [3,8] is left; finally merge [1,5,7,9]+[3,8] → [1,3,5,7,8,9]. Each merge compares the front element of two sorted sub-lists and takes the smaller, building one sorted output.
review_notes: Teacher-marked. Award marks for: divide-into-halves until single-element lists, merge step compares fronts, repeats until one sorted list remains, applied to the example values.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Describe how merge sort would sort the list [9, 5, 7, 1, 3, 8] into ascending order. Use numbered steps and show at least one intermediate stage.

mark_points.text: divide: split the list in half repeatedly until each sub-list has 1 element
conquer/merge: combine pairs of sorted sub-lists by comparing their front elements
repeats until a single sorted list remains
uses the example values to show at least one intermediate state (e.g. [5,9],[1,7],[3,8])
